artists.name: ABIODUN OLAKU
artists.title: ABIODUN OLAKU
artists.bio:
Abiodun Olaku is one of Nigeria's  Master Artists, a most revered Painter and visual storyteller.

He graduated from the prestigious Yaba College of Technology, Lagos, with a  Higher National Diploma in Fine Art in 1981.

At Yaba College, he was privileged to be tutored and mentored  by celebrated master artists, Yusuf Grillo, Kolade Oshinowo, and the late Dr. Isiaka Osunde. Under their tutelage, Olaku developed a uniquely expressive style that has come to define the Yaba School of Art.

The early years of his career was spent in the Nigerian civil service (1982–1989),  but ultimately pursued a career in the arts, establishing his studio at the National Theatre, Lagos in 1989.

A realist painter, Olaku's paintings often depict landscapes, water scenes, figures,night scenes, cultural events, masquerades etc.  …marked by his exceptional draughtsmanship, composition, fascinating depiction of light and shadow and portrayal of space.

His paintings are classic, timeless pieces in a style  reminiscent of the paintings of great European masters such as Rembrandt yet with contemporary and cultural influences, always highlighting the beauty of familiar scenes.

As a founding member of Universal Studios of Arts, National Theatre, Lagos., Olaku has mentored  and inspired over 300 artists.

His career is distinguished by artistic excellence, technical mastery and emotional depth making him a leading figure  in  Nigerian art. Olaku continues to captivate collectors globally, solidifying his status as a Master Artist.

artists.name: BRUCE ONOBRAKPEYA
artists.title: BRUCE ONOBRAKPEYA PhD
artists.bio:
Bruce Onobrakpeya is a legendary Nigerian Master Artist,  Print maker, Painter and Educator whose artistic contributions have shaped modern art across Africa.

He graduated from the prestigious Yaba College of Technology, Lagos, with a  Higher National Diploma in Fine Art in 1981.

At Yaba College, he was privileged to be tutored and mentored  by celebrated master artists, Yusuf Grillo, Kolade Oshinowo, and the late Dr. Isiaka Osunde. Under their tutelage, Olaku developed a uniquely expressive style that has come to define the Yaba School of Art.

Whilst at  the college, the young Onobrakpeya and some of his fellow students protested against the colonial  education curriculum which focused on the European art forms and techniques and ignored the Nigerian culture and tradition. Their dissatisfaction led to them forming the Zaria Art Society and they began a movement which sought to synthesize Nigerian traditional art forms with contemporary techniques.

This earned them the name 'Zaria Rebels' and led to them creating  art that authentically reflected their tradition and experiences.

Onobrakpeya began his career as an art teacher and taught in several schools including the famous St. Gregory's College, Lagos. Whilst teaching at the College, he started an experimental studio practice and soon developed his unique printmaking technique with themes  rooted in his rich Urhobo heritage. His innovative technique  has  attracted scholars,and students globally. He thus redefined the African artist as an originator and not an imitator.

In 1998, he established the Harmattan Workshop in his hometown, Agbarha-Otor and this  has become a hub for nurturing talent and exchanging ideas in the visual arts.

He also founded the Bruce Onobrakpeya Foundation and the Niger Delta Cultural Centre (Onobrak Art Centre).

He is the most documented Nigerian artis.

Dr. Onobrakpeya is a recipient of numerous awards , including the MFR, Living Human Treasure Award, and Pope Paul VI Gold Medal.  His works are in important collections such as the Vatican Museum, Smithsonian Institution, and the National Museum Lagos.

artists.name: FIDELIS ODOGWU
artists.title: FIDELIS ODOGWU
artists.bio:
Fidelis Odogwu is one of Nigeria's Master Sculptors.

He studied Sculpture at the famous Auchi Polytechnic, Auchi, Edo State, Nigeria, graduating in 1991 with a Higher National Diploma in Sculpture.

Therefore he was mentored by renown Nigerian master artists, Ben Osawe and Chris Anyanwu. Their influence helped shape his sculptural style.

His preferred medium is metal and his great skill in transforming ordinary metal to amazing sculptures has earned him the nickname 'Prince of Metal'.

Odogwu's upbringing instilled in him a strong sense of family and a deep appreciation of his tradition and heritage. His sculptures always reflect his culture and he is celebrated for his bold but minimalist fusion of traditional African symbols  with contemporary , sleek,  geometric  and diagonal forms - a synthesization of ancient and modern.

His recent works incorporate paintings on metal, thus blurring the boundaries between painting and sculpture… a technique symbolizing his desire for harmonious co existence.

His themes focus on harmony in families and societies, his way of protesting against political instability and cultural disintegration in our world.

A passionate mentor, he has nurtured many artists and he continues to inspire generations of younger Sculptors.

Odogwu has exhibited extensively across Nigeria and abroad, with his works in significant public and private collections.

artists.name: WALLACE EJOR
artists.title: WALLACE EJOR
artists.bio:
Wallace Ejor is one of Nigeria's accomplished visual artists known for his distinctive style and creative expression.

He graduated from the prestigious Yaba College of Technology, Lagos, with a  Higher National Diploma in Fine Art in 1981.

At Yaba College, he was privileged to be tutored and mentored  by celebrated master artists, Yusuf Grillo, Kolade Oshinowo, and the late Dr. Isiaka Osunde. Under their tutelage, he developed a uniquely expressive style that has come to define the Yaba School of Art.

The early years of his career was spent in the Nigerian civil service (1982–1989),  but ultimately pursued a career in the arts, establishing his studio at the National Theatre, Lagos in 1989.

A versatile artist, his works often depict landscapes, water scenes, figures, night scenes, cultural events, masquerades etc. marked by exceptional draughtsmanship, composition, fascinating depiction of light and shadow and portrayal of space.

His paintings are classic, timeless pieces in a style reminiscent of the paintings of great European masters yet with contemporary and cultural influences, always highlighting the beauty of familiar scenes.

As a founding member of Universal Studios of Arts, National Theatre, Lagos., he has mentored  and inspired many artists.

His career is distinguished by artistic excellence, technical mastery and emotional depth making him a significant figure  in  Nigerian art. He continues to captivate collectors globally, solidifying his status as a Master Artist.
